info.title: mc8yp HTTP API
info.description:
Auxiliary REST surface of the mc8yp Cumulocity MCP microservice.

This spec intentionally documents only the non-MCP HTTP routes (`/refresh-apis`, `/health`). The MCP protocol itself is served at `POST /mcp` over JSON-RPC and is **not** documented here — MCP clients discover tools and prompts via the MCP `tools/list` / `prompts/list` flow, not via OpenAPI. If you are an agent reading this through `serviceSpecs['mc8yp-server']`, you are talking to mc8yp through mc8yp; do not call `/service/mc8yp-server/mcp` from inside `execute`.

The primary use case for this spec is letting an agent trigger a fresh API discovery in mc8yp itself after subscribing or unsubscribing a microservice in the tenant.

post.summary: Bust the API discovery cache and re-run discovery for the calling tenant.
post.description:
Resolves the tenant from the caller's `Authorization` header (or session cookie), busts the per-tenant discovery cache, and synchronously re-runs Cumulocity application + OpenAPI spec discovery using the microservice's service user for that tenant. Returns the freshly discovered spec metadata.

Use this after a new microservice has been subscribed in the tenant — otherwise discovered specs stay cached for 30 minutes.

401.description: No Authorization header or session cookie was provided.

400.description: Could not resolve the calling tenant via `/tenant/currentTenant`.

403.description: Tenant is not subscribed to this microservice.

500.description: Discovery failed for an unexpected reason.

get.description: Returns the static string `OK` with a 200 status when the process is alive. No authentication required.

tenantUrl.description: Base URL of the Cumulocity tenant the microservice is deployed against.

tenantId.description: Cumulocity tenant ID resolved from the caller's auth.

installedContextPaths.description: Context paths of every Cumulocity application currently subscribed by the tenant, regardless of whether the application exposes an OpenAPI spec.

discovered.description: One entry per application that exposes a reachable OpenAPI spec. Paths in each spec have already been prefixed with the application's service URL prefix.

specLabel.description: Human-readable label of the spec entry. Equals the application name for single-spec applications.

pathCount.description: Number of paths declared by the spec after the service-prefix rewrite.
